title: Tapping for Pleasure Guilt
subtitle: An EFT sequence for the guilt that arrives when you enjoy something
excerpt: An EFT tapping practice for the guilt that attaches to pleasure: the feeling that you haven't earned it, that something else should be getting attention, that enjoyment is selfish. Works through the guilt pattern toward a place where pleasure is allowed to exist without a justification.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you notice guilt arriving during or after a moment of pleasure, rest, or enjoyment.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for the guilt that shadows pleasure. The script works through the specific pattern of enjoyment followed by the feeling that the enjoyment was wrong, unearned, or selfish, toward a place where pleasure is allowed to exist without requiring a justification.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though I feel guilty for enjoying myself when there are other things I should be doing, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though pleasure feels like something I have to earn and I never quite earn enough of it, I am open to allowing it anyway.
Even though I have been taught that enjoyment without productivity is selfish, I accept that pleasure is allowed to be part of a life.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: The guilt that shows up when I start to enjoy something.
Side of Eye: The feeling that I haven't done enough to deserve this.
Under Eye: The list of what I should be doing instead.
Under Nose: The voice that says pleasure is selfish.
Chin: Feeling watched and judged for resting.
Collarbone: The difficulty of letting something feel good without immediately pulling away.
Under Arm: Pleasure that gets interrupted by the feeling I don't qualify.
Top of Head: The exhaustion of never being at rest without guilt.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: Pleasure does not require justification. It is allowed.
Side of Eye: I do not have to earn rest and enjoyment. They are part of a sustainable life.
Under Eye: The list will be there after. It is not more urgent than this moment.
Under Nose: The voice that calls pleasure selfish is not telling the truth.
Chin: I am allowed to be in this and let it be good.
Collarbone: Enjoyment is not taken from someone else. It is its own thing.
Under Arm: I let pleasure be what it is, without monitoring it.
Top of Head: I am allowed to enjoy my life. That is not a small thing.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
